revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kei gongs]][[en:Kei (ritual gong)]]
{{Short description|Gong used in Japanese Buddhist practices}}
[[File:Lotus_flower_gong.jpg|thumb|A ''kei'' from the [[:en:Eikan-dō Zenrin-ji|Zenrin-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]]]
{{More citations needed|date=February 2023}}A '''''kei''''' is a [[:en:Gong|Gong]] used in [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhist practice in Japan]].<ref name="Independent Administrative Institution National Museum - Bronze temple gong">{{cite web |title=Bronze temple gong |url=https://emuseum.nich.go.jp/detail?&langId=en&webView=&content_base_id=100449&content_part_id=1 |publisher=[[Independent Administrative Institution National Museum]] |access-date=18 February 2023 |language=English}}</ref> ''Kei'' were adapted from the ancient Chinese instrument known in English as a "[[:en:sounding stone|sounding stone]]"; they share the   [[:en:Chinese character|Chinese character]].<ref name="Independent Administrative Institution National Museum - Bronze temple gong"/>

==References==
{{reflist}}

{{Buddhist temples in Japan}}

{{idiophone-instrument-stub}}
{{Buddhism-stub}}
